title: commelina communis var. communis
intro: Commelina communis var. communis, also known as dayflower or common dayflower, is a plant species within the Commelinaceae family, characterized by its annual or perennial growth habit and its distinctive blue or purple flowers that bloom throughout the day. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Commelina communis var. communis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
